# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains benchmark-design evidence, dataset construction details, quantitative evaluation results for coding agents on repository generation, observed failure patterns, and ablation results on interaction limits and test visibility.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-011
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: method
citation: NL2Repo-Bench, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.12730v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
NL2Repo-Bench evaluates agents that receive one natural-language requirements document and an empty workspace, then must produce a fully installable Python library.

## Source Excerpt

"Given only a single natural-language requirements document and an empty workspace, agents must autonomously design the architecture, manage dependencies, implement multi-module logic, and produce a fully installable Python library."

## Note

The source frames this as the benchmark task definition, not as a performance claim.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-011
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: method
citation: Section 1, benchmark design
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.12730v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The benchmark withholds project scaffolding, source code, and test cases during development and evaluates outputs with the upstream repository's pytest suite in a controlled environment.

## Source Excerpt

"Crucially, no project scaffolding, source code, or test cases are revealed during development..." and "Each generated repository is verified against the original upstream pytest suite of a real-world open-source project, executed within a controlled environment."

## Note

This evidence describes the benchmark's evaluation protocol.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-011
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: metric
citation: Section 3.2, benchmark statistics
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.12730v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The final NL2Repo-Bench dataset contains 104 tasks across nine categories of Python libraries.

## Source Excerpt

"The benchmark comprises 104 tasks spanning nine distinct categories of Python libraries..."

## Note

The category counts are broken out in Table 1.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-011
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: metric
citation: Section 3.2, input length
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.12730v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The average input length of an NL2Repo-Bench task is about 18,800 tokens.

## Source Excerpt

"The average input length of a NL2Repo-Bench task is approximately 18,800 tokens..."

## Note

The source presents this as a benchmark characteristic rather than a model result.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-011
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: method
citation: Section 3.1.1, repository selection
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.12730v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Target repositories were selected using four stated criteria: 300 to 120,000 LOC, at least 10 GitHub stars, passing pytest-based tests in the official version, and creation or update within the past three years.

## Source Excerpt

"Our selection procedure follows four principled criteria..." followed by "Each repository must contain 300 to 120,000 lines of code," "at least 10 GitHub stars," "must include pytest-based test cases, and its official version must successfully pass all of them," and "Only repositories that have been created or updated within the past three years are considered."

## Note

These criteria define the repository pool used to construct tasks.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-011
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: method
citation: Section 3.1.2, specification structure
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.12730v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Each specification document is organized into four sections: Project Description, Supports, API Usage Guide, and Implementation Nodes.

## Source Excerpt

"To standardize the task formulation, every specification document in NL2Repo-Bench is structured into four specific sections:" followed by "Project Description," "Supports," "API Usage Guide," and "Implementation Nodes."

## Note

The source states that this structure is used for every benchmark specification.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-011
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: method
citation: Section 3.1.3, environment building
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.12730v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The benchmark relaxes non-functional build constraints, including README or license-file checks, so evaluation focuses on functional software generation rather than rigid packaging prerequisites.

## Source Excerpt

"We implement a strategy of non-functional constraint relaxation within build configurations." and "mandatory existence checks for auxiliary documentation (such as README.md or license files) are either removed or satisfied via the pre-creation of synthetic artifacts."

## Note

This changes build conditions relative to untouched upstream packaging requirements, so the evaluation isolates functional behavior rather than strict non-code repository completeness.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-011
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: result
citation: Abstract; Section 4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.12730v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The paper reports that long-horizon repository generation remains unsolved, with even the strongest agents staying below about 40.5% average test pass rate.

## Source Excerpt

Abstract: "even the strongest agents achieve below 40% average test pass rates" and Section 4.2: "all models achieve an average test pass rate below 40.5%..."

## Note

This is an aggregate outcome claim about the evaluated systems, not a statement that all repository generation is impossible.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-011
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: metric
citation: Table 3, main results
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.12730v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Claude-Sonnet-4.5 in the Claude Code framework achieved the highest reported overall pass rate at 40.2% with Pass@1 count 3.

## Source Excerpt

Table 3 lists "Claude-Sonnet-4.5 (Claude Code) 40.2 3" and Figure 3 states that Claude-Sonnet-4.5 based agents achieve the highest performance.

## Note

This is the top leaderboard result reported in the source.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-011
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: metric
citation: Table 3, difficulty breakdown
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.12730v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
For Claude-Sonnet-4.5 in Claude Code, pass rate decreases with task difficulty: 51.8% on easy tasks, 44.5% on medium tasks, and 25.1% on hard tasks.

## Source Excerpt

Table 3 reports for "Claude-Sonnet-4.5 (Claude Code)" the difficulty breakdown "51.8 44.5 25.1" for easy, medium, and hard tasks.

## Note

These values are specific to one model-framework combination.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-011
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: claim-support
citation: Section 4.3.1, tool correlation
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.12730v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Among commonly used tools, task_tracker has the strongest reported positive correlation with model performance at 0.711.

## Source Excerpt

"Among the three most frequently used tools, task_tracker exhibits the strongest correlation with model performance (0.711), underscoring the central role of effective task planning..."

## Note

The source presents this as a correlation-based observation about planning-tool usage.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-011
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: incident
citation: Section 4.3.2 and 4.3.3, GPT-5 behavior
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.12730v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
GPT-5 averaged 78.4 interaction turns and was described as frequently halting for user confirmation instead of completing the repository autonomously.

## Source Excerpt

Table 6 reports "GPT-5 78.4" average turns, and Section 4.3.2 states "Our case analysis reveals that GPT-5 frequently halts prematurely and awaits user confirmation..." Section 4.3.3 adds that GPT-5 "tends to halt and wait for user guidance."

## Note

The source interprets this as misalignment with a fully autonomous benchmark, not as a general coding-quality verdict.
```

### file: evidence-13.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-011
evidence_id: evidence-13
kind: incident
citation: Section 4.3.3, early termination and non-finish
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.12730v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Qwen3-Thinking is reported to terminate early in 49.0% of tasks and to have a 46.2% non-finish rate.

## Source Excerpt

Figure 5 discussion states: "Qwen3-Thinking terminates early in 49.0% of tasks." It also says "Thinking models (e.g., Qwen3-T) exhibit an alarmingly high early stop rate of 49.0% and non-finish rate of 46.2%..."

## Note

These rates are presented as failure-pattern statistics for this benchmark setting.
```

### file: evidence-14.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-011
evidence_id: evidence-14
kind: result
citation: Table 8, test-visibility ablation
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.12730v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Revealing all test cases increased Claude-Sonnet-4.5 in Claude Code from 40.2% to 59.4% overall pass rate and from Pass@1 3 to Pass@1 18.

## Source Excerpt

Table 8 reports "Document only ... Overall Avg Score (%) 40.2 Pass@1 3" and "Document + unittest ... Overall Avg Score (%) 59.4 Pass@1 18."

## Note

The source uses this ablation to estimate the benefit of exposing evaluation tests during development.
---
version: extractor-receipt.v1
role: Extractor

evidence_count: 14
completed_at_utc: 2026-04-05T14:06:00Z

### file: evidence-14.md
```markdown
---

article_id: art-2026-03-08-011
evidence_id: evidence-14
kind: result
citation: Table 8, test-visibility ablation
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.12730v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Revealing all test cases increased Claude-Sonnet-4.5 in Claude Code from 40.2% to 59.4% overall pass rate and from Pass@1 3 to Pass@1 18.

## Source Excerpt

Table 8 reports "Document only ... Overall Avg Score (%) 40.2 Pass@1 3" and "Document + unittest ... Overall Avg Score (%) 59.4 Pass@1 18."

## Note

The source uses this ablation to estimate the benefit of exposing evaluation tests during development.

# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains benchmark-design evidence, dataset construction details, quantitative evaluation results for coding agents on repository generation, observed failure patterns, and ablation results on interaction limits and test visibility.

## Evidence Files

---

source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.12730v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
NL2Repo-Bench evaluates agents that receive one natural-language requirements document and an empty workspace, then must produce a fully installable Python library.

## Source Excerpt

"Given only a single natural-language requirements document and an empty workspace, agents must autonomously design the architecture, manage dependencies, implement multi-module logic, and produce a fully installable Python library."

## Note

The source frames this as the benchmark task definition, not as a performance claim.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-011

kind: method
citation: Section 1, benchmark design
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.12730v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The benchmark withholds project scaffolding, source code, and test cases during development and evaluates outputs with the upstream repository's pytest suite in a controlled environment.

## Source Excerpt

"Crucially, no project scaffolding, source code, or test cases are revealed during development..." and "Each generated repository is verified against the original upstream pytest suite of a real-world open-source project, executed within a controlled environment."

## Note

This evidence describes the benchmark's evaluation protocol.
```

## Note
